<!-- version: 1.1.0 -->
# Current PR Contract

This contract constrains implementation scope for the active PR. Update
it when scope is explicitly amended. If a requested action falls outside
approved scope, stop and escalate before proceeding.

## Goal
Bootstrap the cARL runtime into CSPeek and deliver a small, deterministic,
stdlib-only Python CSP scanner (`csp_scanner`) that retrieves
Content-Security-Policy headers for one or more URLs and produces
explainable, rule-based risk assessments with screen/JSON/CSV/SQLite
output.

## Contract status
active

## Non-goals
- No UI layers, dashboards, web APIs, or service daemons.
- No active/noisy subdomain scanning (wordlist DNS resolution only).
- No unbounded crawling.
- No AI/heuristic judgement in risk scoring — rules only.
- No heavyweight frameworks or third-party runtime dependencies.

## Carry-forward rules
- Durable invariants in `.github/carl/invariants.yml` apply unchanged.
- The deterministic rule-based risk model is intended to become a durable
  project truth recorded in `.github/carl/memory.md`.

## Approved scope
- Add `.github/` cARL runtime artefacts generated by `carl init`,
  `carl map`, `carl reconcile`, and `carl harness sync`.
- Add `csp_scanner/` Python package: input handling, HTTP fetch,
  CSP extraction, rule engine, output writers, bounded crawl,
  wordlist subdomain enumeration, CLI.
- Add `tests/` with mocked-HTTP tests (no live network).
- Update `README.md` with usage, output formats, risk model, SQLite
  schema, and limitations.
- Update cARL durable artefacts to record implementation decisions.

## Intentional amendments
- The cARL CLI binary release archive is unavailable (404); the CLI was
  built from source via `go install github.com/goldjg/carl/cmd/carl@latest`
  to achieve the identical result.

## Forbidden scope
- Removing or rewriting the existing LICENSE.
- Adding third-party Python runtime dependencies.
- Any network calls in tests.
- Modifying `.github/carl/runtime.json` manually.

## Architectural constraints
- Standard library only (`urllib`, `argparse`, `sqlite3`, `csv`, `json`,
  `html.parser`, `socket`).
- One module per concern inside `csp_scanner/`.
- Risk rules defined as data (rule table), evaluated deterministically.

## Security constraints
- Bounded network behaviour: timeouts, redirect caps, crawl caps,
  same-origin default for crawling.
- No secrets in code. Validate/normalise URLs; only http/https schemes.
- SSRF-adjacent behaviour must stay opt-in and bounded.

## Files expected to change
- `.github/**` (cARL artefacts)
- `csp_scanner/**` (new)
- `tests/**` (new)
- `README.md`

## Tests / validation
- `python -m unittest discover -s tests -v`
- `python -m py_compile csp_scanner/*.py`
- CLI smoke checks against mocked/local behaviour only.

## Stop conditions
- Any requirement forces a third-party runtime dependency.
- Tests would require live internet access.

## Escalation triggers
- Ambiguity in risk-scoring weights that changes level boundaries.
- Any need to alter LICENSE or repository structure beyond scope.

## Context reset notes
Implementation complete: scanner, CLI, 52 mocked-HTTP tests, README, and
durable artefact updates are done. Close this contract on merge. Follow-up
candidates are recorded under "Open questions" in memory.md (multi-header
CSP merging, meta-tag CSP, strict-dynamic modelling).
